text: vv. 4, 5: The Latin version here inserts a sentence which seems out of place (perhaps belonging to the end of the foregoing chapter); it omits the words given in the Greek text: ‘He who honours his father will atone for his own sins’.
In the Greek text, the giver of alms atones not for his mother’s sin, but for his own. Cf. however Ps. 50.7.
Some manuscripts and versions add, at the end of this verse, ‘Men’s esteem and honour is to be had for the asking, but it is to the humble that hidden things are revealed’.
Some manuscripts and versions add, at the end of this verse, ‘Want eyes, want light; boast not that thou hast knowledge, where knowledge is none’.
